response:
I don't have a file-write capability available in this session — the only tools I can actually call here are `ReportFindings`, `ScheduleWakeup`, `ToolSearch`, `Workflow`, plus `Cron*`, `DesignSync`, `EnterWorktree`/`ExitWorktree`, `Monitor`, `PushNotification`, and `RemoteTrigger`. None of these can create or edit a file like `outputs/generating-release-notes/SKILL.md`. `Bash`, `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, and `Grep` all exist but report "not enabled in this context" when I try them, and there's no generic subagent (`Agent`/`Task`) tool available either.

I could route around this via the `Workflow` tool (its subagents may have normal file tools), but that tool is explicitly gated to cases where you've opted into multi-agent orchestration (e.g. said "use a workflow" or "ultracode"), and this is a straightforward file-authoring task, not something that needs orchestration — so I won't reach for it just to work around a missing tool.

So I can't write the file for you right now. Two ways to unblock:

1. Tell me to go ahead and use a workflow anyway (your explicit ask satisfies the opt-in), and I'll use it purely to get a subagent with file-write access to create the skill.
2. Switch to a session/mode where standard file tools (Read/Write/Edit) are enabled, and I'll write it directly there.

Separately, on the content itself: I read through your documentation, and heads up that a good chunk of it doesn't belong in a SKILL.md as-is — skills are supposed to be lean, actionable trigger+procedure docs, not full reference documentation. Specifically the "History of This Process" section (Confluence → shell script → Priya's rewrite → retirement) is git-tellable backstory with no bearing on how the skill runs today, and the closing marketing line ("ABSOLUTELY AMAZING... HOURS... !!!") isn't something a skill file should contain. I'd fold the "Background" definitions in only if genuinely needed for an agent unfamiliar with semver/git tags, and keep the commit-type mapping and output rules as the operative core. Let me know which unblock option you want, and whether you're fine with me trimming the history/marketing content when I write it.
